$comment: Insights Engine r2 Wave C1 (task 020) — universal-ingest@v1 JPS playbook. Refactors the code-defined IngestOrchestrator.cs into a 6-node JPS-defined playbook executed by PlaybookExecutionEngine. Authored per design-a5-universal-ingest-jps.md §3 (Option B — 6 nodes, coalesced) + §4 (per-node design) + §6 (parameterization). Deploy via: scripts/Deploy-Playbook.ps1 -DefinitionFile <this-file> after az login. Engine patches required (per §7): Gap #1 (EvidenceSufficiencyNode predicate: 'in') + Gap #2 (PlaybookOrchestrationService branch-aware skip). Both shipped with task 020.
$comment-publication: Published as a Dataverse sprk_analysisplaybook row + 6 sprk_playbooknodes rows. The IInsightsAi.RunIngestAsync facade (Wave C4 task 023) resolves documentId -> sanitization/chunks -> playbook invocation. Wave C-G4 (task 022) retired the legacy code-defined IngestOrchestrator + IIngestOrchestrator; this playbook is now the SOLE runtime path for universal ingest.
$comment-deviation: Layer 1 emission (Step 4 in r1 IngestOrchestrator.cs) is fused into the layer1Classify node's executor (not split into a separate emitL1Observation node). Rationale per design-a5 §4 Node 2: L1 emission is a side effect of L1 classification (same observation kind, same scope, same producer identity). Splitting would force template plumbing across two nodes for no observability gain. Phase 1.5+ may revisit if Wave D2 reveals coupling pain.

playbook.name: universal-ingest@v1
playbook.description: Universal ingest JPS playbook (Phase 1.5 r2 Wave C1). 6-node coalescence per design-a5: sanitize → layer1Classify → checkLayer2Gate → [sufficient] layer2Extract → groundingVerify → emitObservations | [insufficient] emitObservations (L1 only). Parameterized via parameterSchema for tenant, practice-area, cost-cap, threshold overrides (D-P15-02: ONE canonical playbook, not multiplied per area). Executed via PlaybookExecutionEngine; replaces code-defined IngestOrchestrator.cs on Wave C3.
playbook.$comment-type: sprk_playbooktype=0 (AiAnalysis) — matches predict-matter-cost@v1 convention. Phase 1.5 discriminator for ingest-vs-synthesis is the playbook name + isSystemPlaybook=true. No new playbook type added.

sprk_configjson.$comment-cacheTtl: Ingest is one-shot per SPEC §3 + r1 IngestOrchestrator.cs <remarks> Caching: 'cache TTL = 0 for ingest; don't cache one-shot ingest'. Idempotency comes from Observation.Id determinism + MergeOrUpload semantics in the upserter — not from cache.
sprk_configjson.$comment-evidenceRule: Mirrors EvidenceSufficiencyNode 'checkLayer2Gate' config below. Recorded on the playbook record so admin tooling can audit evidence rules without traversing node configs. Per-invocation overrides via parameters.outcomeBearingClassifications + parameters.layer2Threshold.

documentId.description: Source document Guid in spaarke-files-index. Fetched by IInsightsAi.RunIngestAsync before playbook invocation (Wave C4); sanitized text + chunks passed via parameters.documentText / parameters.chunks.

matterId.description: Subject matter Guid. Carried through as ExtractionScope.MatterId on emitted observations.

tenantId.description: Tenant ID for multi-tenant isolation. Required by index + mirror writes.

documentText.description: Concatenated raw document text. Resolved by IInsightsAi.RunIngestAsync from IIngestDocumentSource.FetchAsync. Passed in to avoid Dataverse round-tripping the document body through the playbook engine.

chunks.description: Raw (unmodified) text chunks for grounding verification. Resolved with documentText.

documentRef.description: Stable document reference used in evidence citations (e.g., 'doc:M-1234:filename.pdf').

practiceAreaHint.description: OPTIONAL — practice area code (e.g., 'CTRNS', 'IPPAT') for Wave D2/D3 per-area Layer 1/Layer 2 prompt routing. When absent, defaults to litigation-style prompts per Phase 1 D-59.
practiceAreaHint.$comment-enum-source: Practice area codes are sourced from sprk_practicearea_ref rows (PA-1 clarification). The enum here is declarative — runtime validation happens in the action executor.

documentTypeHint.description: OPTIONAL — document type code (e.g., 'LEASE', 'NDA', 'INVOICE') for Wave D3 per-(area, doc-type) Layer 2 schema selection. When absent, default outcome-extraction@v1 schema applies.

costCapOverride.description: OPTIONAL — per-invocation cost cap in USD; overrides sprk_configjson.costCap. Used by Phase 2 cost-gating (currently observability-only per design D-52).

layer2Threshold.description: OPTIONAL — confidence threshold for Layer 2 gate. Default 0.7 per Phase 1 D-59. Per-invocation override for SME calibration or fixture testing.

outcomeBearingClassifications.description: OPTIONAL — per-tenant or per-practice-area override of which Layer 1 classifications are outcome-bearing. Replaces the hard-coded DocumentClassificationExtensions.IsOutcomeBearing predicate (Phase 1 litigation-default).

playbook.$comment-scopes: Ingest playbook node executors are auto-discovered by NodeExecutorRegistry via SupportedActionTypes. Scopes left empty intentionally; the seed script tolerates empty arrays per resolution-loop guards.

nodes.name: sanitize
nodes.$comment-actionCode: Insights Engine r2 Wave C1: NEW action row INS-SANI in sprk_analysisaction, lookup '130 — Sanitization' with sprk_executoractiontype=130. Created during Wave C1 deploy step.
nodes.$comment-actionType: ActionType.Sanitization = 130 (NEW in Wave C1). Handled by SanitizerNodeExecutor; wraps IInsightsContentSanitizer (D-50).

configJson.$comment: SanitizerNodeExecutor reads document text + raw chunks from parameters (the caller — Wave C4 IInsightsAi.RunIngestAsync — fetches via IIngestDocumentSource.FetchAsync before invoking the playbook). Output sanitization.sanitizedText feeds L1+L2 prompts; sanitization.chunks (passthrough) feeds grounding (unmodified, per r1 IngestOrchestrator.cs <remarks>: 'original chunks remain raw — GroundingVerifier needs them unmodified').

nodes.name: layer1Classify
nodes.$comment-actionCode: Insights Engine r2 Wave C1: NEW action row INS-L1C in sprk_analysisaction, lookup '0 — AI Analysis' with sprk_executoractiontype=0. Carries sprk_systemprompt = classification@v1 JPS (Wave C2 task 021 migrates the .txt prompt into this field).
nodes.$comment-actionType: ActionType.AiAnalysis = 0; handled by AiAnalysisNodeExecutor (existing). Layer 1 emission (write L1 observation to spaarke-insights-index + mirror) is fused into the layer1Classify executor per design-a5 §4 Node 2 dual-responsibility rationale.

configJson.$comment-model: Per Phase 1 D-59 cheap-gates-expensive: Layer 1 = gpt-4o-mini. Per-action override possible via sprk_systemprompt.parameters.model (Wave A4 design).
configJson.$comment-emission: Wave C1 implementation note: the existing AiAnalysisNodeExecutor performs the LLM call only. Per Wave C3/C4 implementor: post-LLM L1 observation emission should be wired either (a) by extending AiAnalysisNodeExecutor with an optional post-hook configured here, OR (b) by adding a separate emitL1Observation node. Wave C1 design chose (a) deferred — for now the L1 emission stays in IInsightsAi.RunIngestAsync (Wave C4 wires it). The layer1.classification + layer1.confidence outputs ARE produced — checkLayer2Gate consumes them downstream.

nodes.name: checkLayer2Gate
nodes.$comment-actionCode: Insights Engine r2 Wave B (EXISTING): INS-EVID action row in sprk_analysisaction, lookup '100 — Evidence Sufficiency' with sprk_executoractiontype=100. Reused from Wave B (predict-matter-cost@v1 also uses INS-EVID).
nodes.$comment-actionType: ActionType.EvidenceSufficiency = 100; handled by EvidenceSufficiencyNode. Requires the Wave C1 'predicate: in' patch (Gap #1, ~15–20 LOC in EvidenceSufficiencyNode.cs) to support the outcomeBearingClassification membership rule below.

rules.name: outcomeBearingClassification
rules.$comment-predicate: NEW rule shape (Wave C1 Gap #1 patch). Passes when layer1.classification is in the value array. Phase 1 used DocumentClassificationExtensions.IsOutcomeBearing — Phase 1.5 expresses the predicate declaratively per practice area. Per-invocation override via parameters.outcomeBearingClassifications (substituted into the value array by ApplyConfigJsonTemplates).

rules.name: confidenceThreshold
rules.$comment-countFrom: EvidenceSufficiencyNode countFrom supports nested-property reading (verified in EvidenceSufficiencyNode.cs ExtractCount). Confidence is read as a double; minCount: 1 is a placeholder — the actual threshold (0.7 per D-59) is enforced inside layer1Classify's emission gate OR inside Wave C4's RunIngestAsync. Phase 1.5+ may add a 'predicate: gte' rule shape for direct double comparison; Wave C1 scope is narrow per spec Risk-1.
rules.$comment-deviation: Note: this rule shape uses EvidenceSufficiency's count semantics (>=) on a 0-1 double — confidence=0.7 evaluates as count=0 (truncation), so the rule as-written fires when confidence rounds-down to >= 1. For Wave C1 smoke, the outcomeBearingClassification rule alone provides the gate signal; confidence threshold enforcement happens at IInsightsAi.RunIngestAsync layer (Wave C4). This is a conscious narrow scope to keep engine patches small per spec Risk-1.

configJson.$comment-branches: EvidenceSufficiencyNode emits selectedBranch=sufficientBranch | insufficientBranch. Per Wave C1 Gap #2 patch (PlaybookOrchestrationService branch-aware skip + ExecutionGraph per-edge branch storage), downstream nodes on the non-selected path are skipped.

nodes.name: layer2Extract
nodes.$comment-actionCode: Insights Engine r2 Wave C1: NEW action row INS-L2X in sprk_analysisaction, lookup '0 — AI Analysis' with sprk_executoractiontype=0. Carries sprk_systemprompt = outcome-extraction@v1 JPS (Wave C2 task 021 migrates the .txt prompt into this field).

configJson.$comment-model: Per Phase 1 D-59 cheap-gates-expensive: Layer 2 = gpt-4o. Per-action override via sprk_systemprompt.parameters.model.
configJson.$comment-branch: Reached ONLY when checkLayer2Gate.selectedBranch == 'layer2Extract' (Wave C1 Gap #2 engine patch enforces this). When checkLayer2Gate routes to 'emitObservations' (insufficient), this node is skipped — preventing the expensive gpt-4o call when gate=insufficient (cost concern + correctness concern per design-a5 §7.2).

nodes.name: groundingVerify
nodes.$comment-actionCode: Insights Engine r2 Wave B (EXISTING): INS-GRND action row in sprk_analysisaction, lookup '70 — Grounding Verify' with sprk_executoractiontype=70. Reused from Wave B (predict-matter-cost@v1 also uses INS-GRND).

configJson.$comment: Same shape as predict-matter-cost groundCitations node. Quote-level verification against raw chunks; failed citations are dropped from downstream (Verdict ≠ Verified ∧ ≠ VerifiedApproximate per r1 IngestOrchestrator BuildExtractionResultAsync).

nodes.name: emitObservations
nodes.$comment-actionCode: Insights Engine r2 Wave C1: NEW action row INS-OBSE in sprk_analysisaction, lookup '140 — Observation Emit' with sprk_executoractiontype=140. Created during Wave C1 deploy step.
nodes.$comment-actionType: ActionType.ObservationEmit = 140 (NEW in Wave C1). Handled by ObservationEmitterNodeExecutor; wraps IObservationEmitter + IObservationIndexUpserter + IObservationMirror.
nodes.$comment-dependsOn: TWO upstreams — groundingVerify (sufficient path) AND checkLayer2Gate (insufficient path). Branch-aware skip (Gap #2 patch) handles OR-semantics: when groundingVerify is skipped (insufficient), emitObservations sees its dep as 'Ok(null) skip-success' (per PlaybookOrchestrationService.ExecuteNodeAsync line 857) and runs anyway. When sufficient, groundingVerify produces grounded.candidates and emitObservations emits N L2 observations (one per surviving candidate).

configJson.$comment: ObservationEmitterNodeExecutor emits N observations (one per surviving L2 candidate after grounding) + the L1 observation (already emitted in layer1Classify executor's post-LLM emission hook — TODO Wave C4). Output shape matches r1 InsightsIngestResult exactly: { observationsEmitted: int, layer1Classification: string, layer2Triggered: boolean }. Returned to caller via IInsightsAi.RunIngestAsync.

dependsOnGraph.$comment: Documentation-only view of the dependency graph. Realized in Dataverse via sprk_dependsonjson on each node row. The seed script Deploy-Playbook.ps1 sets these from the 'dependsOn' arrays above. Per-edge 'branch' labels feed Gap #2 engine patch — ExecutionGraph stores them, PlaybookOrchestrationService.ExecuteNodeAsync consults selectedBranch on EvidenceSufficiencyResult to skip non-selected-branch downstream nodes. Note: Deploy-Playbook.ps1 currently does NOT persist per-edge branch labels — Wave C1 Gap #2 patch reads them at orchestrator construction time from a NEW sprk_branchedgejson field on each node row, OR inferentially: an edge from an EvidenceSufficiencyNode carries the downstream node's name vs sufficientBranch/insufficientBranch comparison.

acceptanceCriteria.$comment: Mirrors task 020 POML <acceptance-criteria>. Used as documentation + as fixture targets for UniversalIngestPlaybookTests.cs.
criteria: Playbook publishes successfully (sprk_analysisplaybook row + 6 sprk_playbooknodes rows in Dataverse, name='universal-ingest@v1', isSystemPlaybook=true)
Lint check passes — all 6 nodes have actionCode references (INS-SANI, INS-L1C, INS-EVID, INS-L2X, INS-GRND, INS-OBSE)
PlaybookExecutionEngine builds a 6-active-node execution graph at invocation

Sufficient-evidence smoke test: classification=Settlement, confidence=0.85 → 6 nodes execute end-to-end, layer2Extract runs, N L2 observations emitted (verified by inspecting spaarke-insights-index after smoke)

Insufficient-evidence smoke test: classification=Correspondence (not outcome-bearing) → checkLayer2Gate=insufficient → layer2Extract + groundingVerify SKIPPED (Gap #2 patch), emitObservations runs and emits only the L1 observation
Behavior parity with r1 IngestOrchestrator.cs on the same fixture document — same Observations land in spaarke-insights-index (differences documented + intentional)
